Masters of the Universe🎬

Honestly, I was pleasantly surprised by this one. Going in, I knew basically nothing about the world besides seeing the trailer, which didn’t interest me at all.

The comedy was pretty hit-or-miss, but there was still a goofy charm that worked for me. The action scenes were easily the highlights & where the movie was at its best.

Jared Leto was great as Skeletor. If I didn’t know it was him, I would’ve figured it out the second he started laughing. I kept hearing the Joker.😂

There were some cool side characters, & while a few of the costumes looked pretty silly, the villains actually looked awesome. Usually the bad guys end up looking ugly as hell, but they nailed them here.

It’s nothing amazing, but I had a fun time with it and enjoyed it way more than I expected.

3/5⭐️

Releasing in Theaters This Week🎥

🎬Disclosure Day

🎬The Furious

🍿 🍿

Scary Movie🎬

I’m surprised by how many critics seem focused on this movie not having a plot or feel the need to clarify they weren’t offended by the jokes. They missed the point if you ask me. This is a parody… the real question is whether it’s funny or not. 

Not every joke hit, but the movie takes plenty of big swings, & most of them connect. My sold-out crowd was laughing through almost the entire runtime, me included. I also thought the movie/pop culture references were spot-on, with plenty of jokes that felt current & landed well.

The biggest highlight was seeing Marlon & Shawn Wayans back. Their chemistry remains the heart of the franchise, & they’re absolutely hysterical here. The new characters didn’t do much for me, but the returning cast all got their moments to shine, with plenty of fun surprises along the way.

It’s not the best entry in the franchise, & there are plenty of flaws to point out, but none of them stopped me from having a great time.

3.5/5⭐️

Disclosure Day🎬

Saved by its third act! 
For almost the first two hours, I was interested, but never fully invested. The stakes lacked emotional weight, making much of the buildup feel slower than it needed to be.

That said, once the climax arrives, everything changes.

The payoff absolutely delivers, & the film finally cashes in on its greatest strength: the concept. What makes Disclosure Day so compelling isn’t the reveal itself, but the exploration of what would actually happen if humanity found itself in this situation. The movie places the audience directly into a complex, thought-provoking scenario, & the final act executes it brilliantly.

If only the buildup had been stronger. Too many cheesy moments & obvious teases undercut the suspense, revealing more than they should. I kept wishing for more meaningful dialogue & a few larger-scale, jaw-dropping moments to match the ambition of the premise.

Still, it delivered an experience absolutely worth seeing in theaters & leaves you thinking long after it’s over.

Oh, & Emily Blunt was outstanding.

3.5/5⭐️

The Furious🎬

My gawd… action after action after action! The fight choreography is absolutely insane, delivering some of the most badass mixed martial arts action I’ve seen on the big screen in a long time.

If you’re looking for a deep, layered story, this isn’t it. Girl gets kidnapped, daddy needs to save her. That’s the mission, & the movie never loses its focus.

There are a few absolutely brutal sequences that hit hard! The action stays raw, intense, & genuinely exciting from start to finish.

Sometimes all you need is to sit back & watch a movie kick all kinds of ass. The Furious delivers exactly that.

4/5⭐️

Power Ballad🎬

I enjoyed this one for what it was. The story kept me invested, & the music was the highlight, even if it this movie didn’t do anything particularly new or memorable.

Paul Rudd was a great casting choice & brought plenty of charm to the role, though some of his character’s decisions were a little frustrating at times.

I would’ve preferred a different outcome, but the ending was heartfelt. Overall, it’s an enjoyable, easy watch.

3/5⭐️

Vicious (2025)🎬

This offers a few creepy scenes, but there are too many cheap jump scares that didn’t work. It tries to reinvent a familiar concept, yet never matches the effectiveness of the films that inspired it. 

The central metaphor is more compelling than the actual horror mechanics. After spending so much time focused on the box’s rules, the ending felt disappointingly underwhelming.  “Just stop playing its game,” really, that’s the answer? To me that felt lazy.

Streaming: Paramount+📺

2/5⭐️

Lovely, Dark, and Deep (2023)🎬

By the third act, I honestly didn’t care what was going to happen. The ending itself wasn’t bad, but the entire lead-up to it felt pretty boring.

For a psychological horror film, it never felt scary, unsettling, or even particularly engaging. The tension felt weak from start to finish, & the story never gave me much reason to care. There are some interesting ideas here, but they’re buried under a slow, dull execution.

1.5/5⭐️

The Frenchman’s fifth season at Liverpool saw him finish sixth in the Premier with 124 aerial wins, winning 71.3% of the aerial duels he disputed in 3,100 minutes. To put that into perspective, Madrid’s four centre-backs (Militao, Rudiger, Huijsen, Asencio) won a total of 127 aerial duels COMBINED in La Liga in 2025/2026 in 6,053 minutes, averaging less than two aerial wins per 90 minutes. Konaté has won over 70% of his aerial duels in the Premier League each of the last four seasons, averaging a 71.9% win rate over that time. Hakimi has released a statement denying the allegations: 

✍️ "Justice looked me in the eyes and said to me: 'If you weren't famous, there never would have been a case.' 

I chose to remain silent for years. I thought that staying dignified, being patient, and trusting the justice system would allow the right decisions to be made.

Today, a story that is not mine is being told at the expense of my family, my life, and above all, the truth. Sometimes I feel like I've become an easy target.  I've been waiting for this trial since day one. And now I'm looking forward to it.  Finally, I will be able to speak."

"But Konaté’s physical abilities are not just limited to his height. This past season he registered a maximum sprint speed of 35.3 kilometers per hour in Champions League play, the second fastest on the Liverpool team and the fifth fastest amongst centre-backs in Europe’s top club competition. Only Kylian Mbappé (36.2 km/hr) and Fede Valverde (35.8 km/hr) registered a faster sprint speed than Konaté for Madrid in the Champions last season.

Fast centre-backs have always been highly valued at Madrid as the team dominates possession in the opponents’ half in La Liga and the recovery pace of the centre-backs is instrumental in combatting any possible counter-attacks. Katherine Parkinson reflects on the depiction of marriage in 'RIVALS':

"It's all to do with the context of the time. I was watching the BBC 'PRIDE AND PREJUDICE' with my daughters recently, and sort of trying to explain to them the stakes for young women getting married back in those days. The '80s isn't as distant in the past, but it was similar in that, if you weren't financially independent, you were maybe more trapped in a bad marriage than you would be, hopefully, today. So I think it's a way of looking back at the '80s, and then missing some things — but also realising how much better things have got for women."

Toy Story 5🎬

What an awesome, fun adventure.🤠 This franchise just can’t miss. The story centers around toys competing with technology, which sounds a little cliché, but Pixar finds plenty of heart & humor in the idea.

I was surprised by how funny it was. A lot of the jokes & fast-paced dialogue felt like they were written just as much for adults as for kids. Some of my favorite moments involved the group of Buzz Lightyears sneaking around & slowly realizing they’re toys, definitely some of the movie’s funniest moments.

I do wish Rex & Mr. Potato Head had a little more to do, but overall I had a great time with it. And that post-credit scene? Pure magic.

4/5⭐️

🇭🇹  Grenadye, alaso! – Grenadiers, forward! 

Haitians tend to have a saying for almost everything, many of which relate to moments in the country’s history in which they have found a way to fight back or stand together in the face of difficulty.

Nou la toujou. We are still here.

Tout moun se moun. Every person is a person.

Nou se wozo, nou pliye nou pa kase. We are like a reed. We bend, we do not break. 

It is why the rallying cry around the national team — Grenadye, alaso!, which means, in Haitian Creole, “Grenadiers, forward!” — feels more fitting than ever before. 

Due to turmoil and gang violence in the country, Haiti’s path to its first World Cup appearance since 1974 came without one single home qualifier. Yet, the island nation pushed its way through the Concacaf process, finishing top of a group featuring recent qualifiers Costa Rica and Honduras.

🚨 Which new rule introduced at this World Cup has had the biggest impact on the game so far?

• Any player seen covering their mouth in a confrontation may be given a red card.

• If a player leaves the pitch in protest, this may result in a red card.

• A red card will be given to any player or team official that encourages or orders the players to leave the pitch in protest of a decision by the referee.

• If a referee feels a goal kick is being deliberately delayed, it will turn into a corner for the opposing team if it takes more than 5 seconds.

• The same 5 second countdown applies to throw-ins.

• When a player is being subbed out, the player must leave the pitch in under 10 seconds. If the player does not do so, the one who replaces him will have to wait for the next stoppage of play, with a minimum of 1 minute.

• Players requiring medical treatment must leave the pitch immediately, and can only return after 1 minute off the pitch.

• VAR is now permitted to check and review red cards from clearly incorrect second yellow cards, mistaken identities, incorrectly awarded corner kicks, and clear attacking team offenses before the ball is in play.
